title: Prim, Kruskal, Wilson, Bias, and Distribution Claims · Runtime Lens: Randomized Prim
summary: Algorithms are judged from one attractive screenshot, “random” is called uniform, and difficulty is inferred from generation time. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the randomized prim increment in comparative seeded generation laboratory. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Generate a spanning tree from a randomized frontier while preventing duplicate or stale frontier choices.
Verify all cells visited, edge count, reciprocal walls, connectivity, acyclicity, seed replay, and changed dimensions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-randomized-prim — Generate a spanning tree from a randomized frontier while preventing duplicate or stale frontier choices.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-generation-alternatives-bias-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-generation-alternatives-bias-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_generation_alternatives_bias_2(seeds: int = 64, valid_trees: int = 64, algorithms: int = 4, measured_metrics: int = 5, uniformity_claim_bounded: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if seeds < 16 or valid_trees < 0:
        return False, valid_trees, "sample-budget"
    if valid_trees != seeds:
        return False, valid_trees, "invalid-generated-tree"
    if algorithms < 4 or measured_metrics < 4:
        return False, algorithms, "comparison-coverage"
    if not uniformity_claim_bounded:
        return False, measured_metrics, "distribution-overclaim"
    assert valid_trees == seeds
    return True, valid_trees, "bounded-generator-comparison"

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Generate a spanning tree from a randomized frontier while preventing duplicate or stale frontier choices.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Randomized Prim model · Theory Randomized Prim, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Randomized Prim precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Randomized Prim model · Theory Randomized Prim, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3415 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. Build deterministic Python evidence to compare multiple generators across seeded topology and bias evidence without unsupported distribution claims; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate a spanning tree from a randomized frontier while preventing duplicate or stale frontier choices.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Randomized Prim model · Theory Randomized Prim, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Randomized Prim is the same procedure and distribution as depth-first backtracking.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-randomized-prim-b9hvxy.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-randomized-prim-b9hvxy(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_randomized_prim_b9hvxy\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?seeds < 16 or valid_trees < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?valid_trees != seeds)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?algorithms < 4 or measured_metrics < 4)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not uniformity_claim_bounded)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
